definition: ACM and Kim-Wright term premium models diverge by 30-50bp on estimates of 50-100bp, creating signal-to-noise ratios approaching 1:1. This operationally significant uncertainty propagates through all downstream yield curve analysis.
evidence: Arithmetic on published model outputs: 30-50bp divergence on 50-80bp signal
The 30-50bp divergence maps to the difference between interest coverage of 1.5x (survivable) and 1.2x (distressed) for sub-IG refinancing
All four debates reinforced this finding without challenge
open_questions: Can ensemble methods or model-averaging reduce the divergence to operationally acceptable levels?
How should downstream analysis propagate the ±30-50bp irreducible uncertainty range?
